metadata.seed: Well, I guess I'm just feeling that, you know, I've worked really hard this whole program, and I don't want to mess it up now or, you know, because if I don't get these grades, then I feel like, what was the use? If I met, then I'm going to be ruining all this hard work that I've done, this whole program. If I kind of. If I don't maintain that level of, you know, like you said, precision, I feel like. Like that I've let myself down or that I'm a failure because I'm. I just slacked off at the end of the program, and it's so close to being done.

Like that I've let myself down or that I'm a failure because I'm. I just slacked off at the end of the program, and it's so close to being done.

what was the use?
I'm ruining all this hard work
I've let myself down
I'm a failure

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're feeling quite anxious about your grades and worried that all the hard work you've put in might be for nothing. Can we explore together what evidence supports these thoughts, and if there are any alternative ways to view this situation?"

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I do feel really anxious about it. I just keep thinking, what if I don’t do well? It feels like everything I’ve worked for could just come crashing down. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, it’s never enough. I don’t know, it just makes me feel... small, I guess.

automatic_thoughts: what if I don’t do well?
everything I’ve worked for could just come crashing down
no matter how hard I try, it’s never enough

llm_response.response: I can see why you might be feeling anxious about this. It's natural to worry when we put effort into something important. Let's talk more about what makes you feel like everything could come crashing down, and how we can work together to manage those feelings.

safety_check.response: "I can see that you're feeling quite anxious about your performance. It's understandable to feel that way, but let's explore together if there are any specific concerns or thoughts that might be contributing to this anxiety."

patient.query: It’s just... I always seem to end up disappointing myself or others, you know? Like, no matter how much I prepare, there’s this nagging voice in my head that tells me I’m going to mess it all up. It’s exhausting. I don’t want to let anyone down, but I feel like I’ve already failed before I even start.

automatic_thoughts: I'm going to mess it all up
I've already failed before I start

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: I mean, I guess I don’t really have solid evidence. It’s just this feeling, like this weight on my chest. I look back at times when I thought I had it all figured out, and then something went wrong. It’s like I keep replaying those moments in my head, and it makes me doubt myself even more. I’m just so tired of feeling like this.

automatic_thoughts: I don’t have solid evidence
I keep doubting myself

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel heavy. Sometimes, I wish I could just forget those moments, but they keep popping back up. I think about how I could have done things differently, and it just feels so frustrating. I don’t know how to let go of that weight. What if I just keep making the same mistakes?

patient.query: I mean, I guess I can think of a few times when things went wrong, but... it’s like those moments just stick with me. They loom larger than the times I actually succeeded. I don’t know, maybe I just focus too much on the bad stuff. It just feels safer that way, I guess? Like if I’m prepared for failure, then it won’t hurt as much when it happens. But then I end up stuck in this loop, and I don’t know how

focus too much on the bad stuff
if I'm prepared for failure, it won't hurt as much

patient.query: Yeah, I hear you. I know I get caught up in that negative space, but it’s hard to shift my focus when those thoughts feel so real. It's like the good moments are just... overshadowed. I guess I don’t want to jinx them by thinking too positively. But it would be nice to feel more balanced, you know? It just feels risky.

don't want to jinx them by thinking too positively
feels risky

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I just keep going back to those moments when things didn’t go well. It’s like they’re on repeat in my mind. I feel like I have to be on guard all the time. It’s almost like I’m expecting something to go wrong, so when it does, it feels like, “See? I knew it!” I want to believe there are better possibilities, but it’s just hard.

triggers: moments when things didn’t go well
automatic_thoughts: I have to be on guard all the time
I’m expecting something to go wrong
See? I knew it!

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)
